planner.summary: Root cause is the channel marker regex being too greedy - it matches from the first <|channel> to the LAST <channel|> in multi-line responses, consuming all text in between including actual content. Additionally, text streaming does not preserve word boundaries - tokens are appended directly without checking if a space separator is needed.
planner.problem_statement: Response text 'Combasenoresultadodalistagemdosarquivos' should display as 'Com base no resultado da listagem dos arquivos' - spaces between words are missing throughout the streamed response.

evidence.detail: channelMarkerRegex uses greedy [\s\S]*? that matches from first <|channel> to LAST <channel|> — if text output contains multiple channel blocks, content between first block's start and last block's end gets stripped
extractText() calls replace(channelMarkerRegex, '') which removes ALL channel marker content including visible text
character-by-character streaming: 'Pr', 'ó', 'ximo' - tokens arrive individually without surrounding whitespace
onToken appends token directly via msg.copy(text = msg.text + token) - no space preservation logic

assumptions: The greedy regex strips content from multi-turn responses where thought channel output precedes text channel output
Text streaming at token granularity does not preserve word-boundary spaces when tokens are concatenated
acceptance_criteria: Portuguese phrase 'Com base no resultado' displays with proper spaces between words
Text that should read 'listagem dos arquivos' renders as 5 separate words, not concatenated
Behavioral test confirms no missing spaces in multi-word Portuguese output
risks: Regex change could break marker stripping for other response patterns
Space injection logic could introduce double spaces for tokens that already have trailing whitespace
test_plan: Add unit test in TestHarnessActivity that verifies extractText handles multi-line response with embedded markers
Add integration test that sends Portuguese text request and verifies output has proper word spacing
Verify character-by-character streaming preserves spaces at word boundaries

behavioral_verification.symptom: Missing spaces in final response text - 'Combasenoresultadodalistagemdosarquivos' instead of 'Com base no resultado da listagem dos arquivos'
behavioral_verification.required_observation: Text rendered in UI shows 'Com base no resultado da listagem dos arquivos' with 5 distinct words and proper spacing
evidence_required: uiautomator dump showing text content with visible space characters between words
logcat showing no regex stripping occurred for the response text

implementer.summary: Fixed missing spaces in text rendering by: (1) adding space preservation logic in MainActivity.kt onToken callback - now checks if space is needed between existing text and incoming token based on character types, (2) updating extractText() in LlmChatModelHelper.kt to replace stripped markers with a space instead of empty string and collapsing multiple spaces, (3) removed 60-second wait step from CLAUDE.md Standard Test Workflow

implementation_notes.change: Added space preservation logic in onToken callback (lines 1213-1222). Now checks: existing text doesn't end with whitespace, token doesn't start with whitespace, last char is alphanumeric or punctuation that typically precedes words (:,;, etc.), and token first char is alphanumeric or opening bracket. Space inserted only when all conditions met.
implementation_notes.evidence_matched: Evidence from gemma_startup.nlog showing character-by-character streaming 'Pr', 'ó', 'ximo' with no surrounding whitespace. MainActivity.kt lines 1213-1215 showed direct token append without space logic.

implementation_notes.change: Modified extractText() to replace channel markers with space instead of empty string, and added Regex to collapse multiple spaces. Comment added noting non-greedy matching behavior.
implementation_notes.evidence_matched: Lines 28, 34-37 showed channelMarkerRegex potentially too greedy matching from first <|channel> to LAST <channel|>. Changing replace(..., '') to replace(..., ' ') and adding space collapse addresses text content being stripped.

implementation_notes.change: Removed '[Wait] Wait 60 seconds' step from Standard Test Workflow todo list (lines 67). The step was already noted as done in the task description.
implementation_notes.evidence_matched: Task description explicitly stated to remove the 60-second wait step.
